rqs: What is the time complexity of QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, and TimSort compared to Insertion Sort, Selection Sort, and Bubble Sort in academic journals?
Which sorting algorithm provides the best space efficiency for software developers when processing large datasets in benchmark suites?
In which scenarios do stable sorting algorithms like TimSort outperform unstable ones such as QuickSort for data engineers in industrial case studies?
base_query: ("Software Developers" OR "Algorithm Researchers" OR "Data Engineers" OR "Computer Science Students" OR "Embedded Systems" OR "Researcher") AND ("QuickSort" OR "MergeSort" OR "HeapSort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "TimSort") AND ("Insertion Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Bubble Sort" OR "Shell Sort" OR "Counting Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Stability" OR "Comparisons" OR "Swaps") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industrial Case Studies")
arxiv_query: (all:"Software Developers" OR all:"Algorithm Researchers" OR all:"Data Engineers" OR all:"Computer Science Students" OR all:"Embedded Systems" OR all:"Researcher") AND (all:"QuickSort" OR all:"MergeSort" OR all:"HeapSort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"TimSort") AND (all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort" OR all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Shell Sort" OR all:"Counting Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity" OR all:"Space Complexity" OR all:"Stability" OR all:"Comparisons" OR all:"Swaps") AND (all:"Academic Journals" OR all:"Conference Proceedings" OR all:"Open Source Repositories" OR all:"Benchmark Suites" OR all:"Industrial Case Studies")

include: Indexed in Scopus or Web of Science
High citation count (>50) or venue rank (A*)
